title: LowerTypes is growing and needs some factoring
body:
A couple high-level points:
* Struct lowering and vector lowering should be separate as we may want to do one and not the other.  Specifically, I imagine vector lowering won't be something that we enable normally.
* There is a difference between dynamic indexes and everything else as dynamic indexes cause mux trees at the access op, where-as static indexes and field accesses are trivial replacements of the op with the lowered values.

We've been talking about restructuring things so that a single layer of a type is peeled at a time.  We've debated either doing all the logic at point of complex type value creation (args, instances) or doing all the work at use-points (index ops).

This is a tracking issue so we don't lose this and we can get discussion on it.
